title: The knowledge economy and academic capitalism
abstract: Click to increase image sizeClick to decrease image size Notes 1. See, for example, Slaughter, and Leslie (1997 Slaughter, S. and Leslie, L. 1997. Academic capitalism: Politics, policies and the entrepreneurial university, Baltimore, MD: Johns‐Hopkins University Press. [Google Scholar]). 2. See, for example, Altbach et al. (1999 Altbach, P., Berdahl, R.O. and Gumport, P.J., eds. 1999. American higher education in the twenty‐first century: Social political and economic challenges, Baltimore, MD: Johns Hopkins University Press. [Google Scholar]). 3. See, for example, Guillory (1993 Guillory, J. 1993. Cultural capital: The problem of literary canon formation, Chicago: University of Chicago Press. [Crossref] , [Google Scholar]) and Readings (1996 Readings, B. 1996. The university in ruins, Cambridge, MA: Harvard University Press. [Google Scholar]). 4. See, for example, Washburn (2005 Washburn, J. 2005. University, Inc: The corporate corruption of American higher education, New York: Basic Books. [Google Scholar]), Brint (2002 Brint, S., ed. 2002. The future of the city of intellect: The changing American university, Stanford, CA: Stanford University Press. [Crossref] , [Google Scholar]), and Kirp (2003 Kirp, D. 2003. Shakespeare, Einstein, and the bottom line, Cambridge, MA: Harvard University Press. [Google Scholar]). 5. See Maher and Tetreault (2007 Maher, F.A. and Tetreault, M.K. 2007. Privilege and diversity in the academy, New York: Routledge. [Google Scholar]).
authors_text: Maher, Frances A.; Tetreault, Mary Kay
journal: British Journal of Sociology of Education
